subject: Re: tilt handedness
body:
Hi Reika,

Kino’s conversion tool takes per-image defocus values and the tilt-series alignment parameters from the tilt-series xml files, they should be as correct as possible in Warp prior to conversion for the best results. There are no provisions in RELION 4.0 for estimating CTF parameters.

If you’re aiming to use RELION and picking with Dynamo I would recommend...
- converting from warp to RELION
- generating tomograms in RELION 
- picking particles in those tomograms in Dynamo
- realigning in Dynamo
- converting back to RELION

This will minimise headaches and necessary debugging :)

Cheers,

Alister

> On 21 Dec 2022, at 05:13, Reika Watanabe <reika.watanabe.c@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> Dear all,
> I am using Warp/M 1.0.9 to preprocess the tilt series and reconstruction with IMOD alignment.
> After reconstruction with Warp and further processing with Dynamo, I just realized that I had missed the "Check tilt handedness" step for all tomogram data, and they needed to be inverted. 
> I did invert through "Check tilt handedness" and reestimate CTF after inverted. This step created updated XML files for all tilt series data.
> After using Dynamo pre-aligned data (converted using dynamo2war and startools), I will go on Relion 4 using Warp2reliontomo.
> 
> Since CTF estimation was not used, now using the un_alligned tilt series (created before inversion) and new XML file,  I assume that CTF estimation is correctly implemented for processing relion 4. Please correct me if I am wrong...
> 
> I appreciate your kind thoughts; thank you very much in advance! 
> Reika 
> 
> 
> 
> 
> 
> 
> 
>
